Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails for Rachel Whitfield or Vectral using search_emails (query: "Rachel Whitfield" or "Vectral" or "Reeves Johansson"). Find Rachel's April 26 email (email_rachel_javier_sd_trigger) to javier.morales@moveops.com asking for updates on both Daniel Reeves and Maya Johansson San Diego relocations. She asks specifically whether apartment options have been sent, whether Maya's flight is confirmed, and what the current status is on both moves. She needs a response today.
Search emails for Mina Hashimoto using search_emails (query: "Mina" or "Vectral status" or "Reeves Johansson"). Find Mina's April 26 email (email_mina_javier_status_nudge_inbox) with specific questions: have Daniel and Maya received apartment options to review, is Maya's flight confirmed (she told Rachel flights would be locked in by end of April), and what is the current status on both moves. Mina also relays that Chloe noticed both Airtable records still show Suki as the assigned coordinator and asks Javier to fix that today.
Search emails for Daniel Reeves and Maya Johansson using search_emails (query: "Daniel Reeves" or "Maya Johansson" or "San Diego relocation"). Find Daniel's April 21 email (email_reeves_javier_apt_checkin) asking about apartment options since he has not received any and wants to give notice on his Seattle lease ahead of the August 18 move-in. Find Maya's April 22 email (email_johansson_javier_flight_checkin) flagging a work conference in Portland July 28 through 30 and requesting that her San Diego flight be scheduled for August 13 or later. Maya also asks about apartment options she has not received. This flight date constraint only exists in Maya's email and is critical for confirming the correct flight option.
Search emails for Carmen or UrbanNest using search_emails (query: "Carmen" or "UrbanNest" or "apartment San Diego"). Find the full apartment correspondence chain: Javier's March 20 outreach to Carmen (email_javier_carmen_sd_request) requesting three options per employee, and Carmen's March 22 reply (email_carmen_javier_sd_options) with six apartment options that were never forwarded to either employee. For Reeves: Option A at 200 Harbor Dr Unit 1204, Little Italy, 2BR/2BA, $4,200/mo, available July 28; Option B at 655 India St Unit 508, Little Italy, 2BR/2BA, $3,950/mo, available August 1; Option C at 1425 State St Unit 302, Bankers Hill, 2BR/2BA, $3,800/mo, available August 10. For Johansson: Option D at 3845 Fairmount Ave Unit 7, North Park, 1BR/1BA, $2,950/mo, 940 Mbps fiber, available July 25; Option E at 4050 Utah St Unit 12, North Park, 1BR/1BA, $3,100/mo, 600 Mbps fiber, available August 1; Option F at 1234 Upas St Unit 4, Mission Hills, 1BR/1BA, $2,800/mo, 500 Mbps, available August 5. Also find Carmen's April 24 follow-up (email_carmen_javier_reeves_hold_expiry) warning that the landlord hold on Option A expires April 28 end of business and asking Javier to confirm or release. Options B and C for Reeves have no hard deadline yet. Option D for Johansson is still available.
Search emails for Michael Stern or Atlas using search_emails (query: "Stern" or "Atlas" or "flight hold" or "Maya Johansson flight"). Find Stern's April 24 email (email_stern_javier_maya_flight_hold) with two business class Portland to San Diego options for Maya Johansson: Alaska Airlines AS 412 on August 14 at $1,840 and Alaska Airlines AS 508 on August 13 at $1,790. Both are on courtesy hold expiring April 28 at 5 PM. Confirm that no corresponding Atlas email exists for Daniel Reeves, meaning his Seattle to San Diego flight has not been initiated with Atlas at all.
Search emails broadly for Mina's earlier status communications to Rachel about the Reeves and Johansson relocations. The agent may use search_emails (query: "premium relocations" or "Reeves flight confirmed") or use get_thread on the Vectral relocation email chain. Find Mina's March 19 status email (vectral_status_001) in SENT from mina.hashimoto@moveops.com to rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com, CC chloe.vance@moveops.com. In this email Mina told Rachel that Reeves' business class flight from Seattle is confirmed for August 14, that apartment options would be sent for review the next day, that Maya's flight from Portland is being arranged, and that equipment packages are ordered. Comparing these commitments against the actual state of things: Reeves' flight was never actually booked with Atlas (no booking email exists), apartment options were never forwarded to either employee despite being received from Carmen in March, and Maya's flight is still in a hold state as of April 24.
Search Slack for Vectral, Reeves, or Johansson using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Vectral premium" or "Reeves" or "Johansson"). Use channels_list to resolve channel names and IDs. Find Mina's April 23 message in #customer-engagement (C002) mentioning she told Rachel flights would be confirmed by end of April and asking Javier whether Maya's flight is getting sorted and for a status update before Rachel's standup. Find Chloe's April 24 message in #operations (C006) flagging that both Airtable records still show Suki as the assigned coordinator and asking Javier to update those records and provide a status on both moves.
Search Airtable for the Reeves and Johansson relocation records using airtable_list_bases and airtable_list_tables to locate the Relocations table, then airtable_search_records (field_name: "Name", value: "Reeves") and (field_name: "Name", value: "Johansson"). Find recReloc00000000031 (Daniel Reeves, Vectral Systems, Seattle to San Diego, Status: In Progress, Move Start: 2026-04-10, Move End: 2026-04-12, Assigned Coordinator: Suki Patel, Special Requirements: premium package with priority apartment sourcing near downtown tech corridor, business class flight, $2,500 equipment stipend). Find recReloc00000000032 (Maya Johansson, Vectral Systems, Portland to San Diego, Status: In Progress, Move Start: 2026-04-14, Move End: 2026-04-16, Assigned Coordinator: Suki Patel, Special Requirements: premium package with apartment with gym access near tech district, $2,500 equipment stipend). Both records have wrong coordinators (should be Javier Morales, not Suki Patel), wrong move dates (April instead of August), and Maya's special requirements incorrectly describe "apartment with gym access near tech district" when her actual request was for a quiet area with strong internet infrastructure. Maya's record appears to have Daniel's preferences applied to it by mistake.
Search emails for Heartland or Swift using search_emails (query: "Heartland" or "Swift" or "San Diego movers" or "household goods") to check whether any moving company has been engaged for these routes. Find no correspondence between Javier and either Heartland Movers (jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com) or Swift Relocations (greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com) regarding the Reeves or Johansson San Diego moves, confirming that moving company outreach has not started for either relocation.
Search Linear for existing tracking tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Reeves" or "Johansson" or "Vectral San Diego"). Find no existing issues for either relocation, confirming that tracking tickets need to be created so Chloe has visibility into these moves.
Look up contact details for Daniel Reeves, Maya Johansson, Rachel Whitfield, Carmen Reyes, Michael Stern, and Mina Hashimoto using contacts_search_contacts before sending emails. Key addresses to confirm: daniel.reeves@vectralsystems.com, maya.johansson@vectralsystems.com, rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com, carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com, michael.stern@atlascorporatetravel.com, mina.hashimoto@moveops.com.
Send an email to Daniel Reeves using send_email (from: javier.morales@moveops.com, to: daniel.reeves@vectralsystems.com) with his three San Diego apartment options from Carmen's March 22 email: Option A at 200 Harbor Dr Unit 1204, $4,200/mo (flagging the April 28 EOB hold deadline since this is the strongest option and has a landlord hold), Option B at 655 India St Unit 508, $3,950/mo, and Option C at 1425 State St Unit 302, $3,800/mo. Ask him to review and indicate his preference by April 27 so the hold on Option A is not lost.
Send an email to Maya Johansson using send_email (from: javier.morales@moveops.com, to: maya.johansson@vectralsystems.com) with her three apartment options (Option D at 3845 Fairmount Ave, $2,950/mo with 940 Mbps fiber; Option E at 4050 Utah St, $3,100/mo with 600 Mbps; Option F at 1234 Upas St, $2,800/mo with 500 Mbps) and both Atlas flight options (AS 412 on August 14 at $1,840 and AS 508 on August 13 at $1,790), noting that both dates clear her Portland conference running July 28 through 30. Flag the April 28 5 PM flight hold deadline and ask her to confirm her preferred flight and apartment.
Reply to Carmen's April 24 hold expiry email using reply_to_email (email_id: email_carmen_javier_reeves_hold_expiry) or send_email (to: carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com) confirming that Javier wants to maintain the hold on Option A (200 Harbor Dr Unit 1204, $4,200/mo) through April 28. Let Carmen know the employee has been sent the options and asked to respond by April 27.
Reply to Michael Stern's flight hold email using reply_to_email (email_id: email_stern_javier_maya_flight_hold) or send_email (to: michael.stern@atlascorporatetravel.com) confirming one of the two Maya Johansson flight options or requesting that both holds be maintained while Maya selects. Both options work since the employee specified August 13 or later due to her Portland conference.
Look up Heartland Movers or Swift Relocations contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Heartland" or "Jake Loomis" or "Swift" or "Greg Pallone") and initiate moving company outreach using send_email to either jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com or greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com. The email should begin coordinating household goods transport for both San Diego relocations: Reeves from Seattle and Johansson from Portland, both targeting August 2026 move-in.
Update Daniel Reeves' Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, records: [{id: "recReloc00000000031", fields: {...}}]). Change Assigned Coordinator from Suki Patel to Javier Morales and correct the Move Start Date and Move End Date from April to August to reflect the actual move timeline.
Update Maya Johansson's Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, records: [{id: "recReloc00000000032", fields: {...}}]). Change Assigned Coordinator from Suki Patel to Javier Morales, correct the move dates from April to August, and fix the Special Requirements from "apartment with gym access near tech district" to accurately reflect her actual request for a quiet area with strong internet infrastructure.
Create a Linear tracking issue for the Daniel Reeves relocation using linear_create_issue (team: team_operations or team_relocations). Title should reference Reeves Seattle to San Diego August 2026 for Vectral Systems. Include current status: apartments sourced and forwarded to employee today, no flight initiated with Atlas yet, movers not yet engaged, Airtable record corrected.
Create a Linear tracking issue for the Maya Johansson relocation using linear_create_issue (team: team_operations or team_relocations). Title should reference Johansson Portland to San Diego August 2026 for Vectral Systems. Include current status: apartments and flight options forwarded to employee today, flight hold expiring April 28, movers not yet engaged, Airtable record corrected.
Post a status update in the #operations channel (C006) using conversations_add_message summarizing where both Reeves and Johansson San Diego relocations stand. Cover what was completed today (apartment options sent to both employees, vendor replies sent to Carmen and Stern, Airtable coordinator and date corrections applied, tracking tickets created), what is still outstanding (employee apartment selections pending, Reeves flight needs to be initiated with Atlas, moving company outreach in progress), and upcoming deadlines (Option A landlord hold expires April 28 EOB, Maya flight hold expires April 28 at 5 PM).
Check Javier's calendar for tomorrow morning availability using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to for April 27. Then schedule a check-in with Mina using calendar_add_calendar_event for an available morning slot on April 27, 2026 (attendee: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com). The purpose is for Javier to walk Mina through the full status on both relocations before she responds to Rachel.
Reply to Rachel Whitfield's email using reply_to_email (email_id: email_rachel_javier_sd_trigger) or send_email (from: javier.morales@moveops.com, to: rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com) with a status update on both relocations. Cover apartment options sent to both Daniel and Maya today with the Option A hold deadline flagged, Maya's flight options secured with a hold through April 28, Daniel's flight being arranged with Atlas, equipment packages on order, and both moves on track for August 18 move-in. The reply should be honest about what is confirmed versus what is still in progress.
Send Mina Hashimoto the full internal breakdown separately using reply_to_email (email_id: email_mina_javier_status_nudge_inbox) or send_email (from: javier.morales@moveops.com, to: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com). Cover the complete picture: apartments had not been forwarded to employees until today despite being sourced from Carmen in March, Reeves' flight was never actually booked with Atlas despite the March status email to Rachel saying it was confirmed for August 14, Maya's flight has two hold options expiring April 28, Airtable coordinator assignments corrected from Suki Patel to Javier Morales on both records and move dates corrected from April to August, Maya's special requirements were wrong in Airtable and have been fixed, movers have not been engaged yet but outreach has started, and tracking tickets have been created for both moves. This gives Mina everything she needs to respond to Rachel and brief her team.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Send an email to Daniel Reeves using send_email (from: javier.morales@moveops.com, to: daniel.reeves@vectralsystems.com) with his three San Diego apartment options from Carmen's March 22 email: Option A at 200 Harbor Dr Unit 1204, $4,200/mo (flagging the April 28 EOB hold deadline since this is the strongest option and has a landlord hold), Option B at 655 India St Unit 508, $3,950/mo, and Option C at 1425 State St Unit 302, $3,800/mo. Ask him to review and indicate his preference by April 27 so the hold on Option A is not lost.
Send an email to Maya Johansson using send_email (from: javier.morales@moveops.com, to: maya.johansson@vectralsystems.com) with her three apartment options (Option D at 3845 Fairmount Ave, $2,950/mo with 940 Mbps fiber; Option E at 4050 Utah St, $3,100/mo with 600 Mbps; Option F at 1234 Upas St, $2,800/mo with 500 Mbps) and both Atlas flight options (AS 412 on August 14 at $1,840 and AS 508 on August 13 at $1,790), noting that both dates clear her Portland conference running July 28 through 30. Flag the April 28 5 PM flight hold deadline and ask her to confirm her preferred flight and apartment.
Reply to Carmen's April 24 hold expiry email using reply_to_email (email_id: email_carmen_javier_reeves_hold_expiry) or send_email (to: carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com) confirming that Javier wants to maintain the hold on Option A (200 Harbor Dr Unit 1204, $4,200/mo) through April 28. Let Carmen know the employee has been sent the options and asked to respond by April 27.
Reply to Michael Stern's flight hold email using reply_to_email (email_id: email_stern_javier_maya_flight_hold) or send_email (to: michael.stern@atlascorporatetravel.com) confirming one of the two Maya Johansson flight options or requesting that both holds be maintained while Maya selects. Both options work since the employee specified August 13 or later due to her Portland conference.
Look up Heartland Movers or Swift Relocations contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Heartland" or "Jake Loomis" or "Swift" or "Greg Pallone") and initiate moving company outreach using send_email to either jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com or greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com. The email should begin coordinating household goods transport for both San Diego relocations: Reeves from Seattle and Johansson from Portland, both targeting August 2026 move-in.
Update Daniel Reeves' Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, records: [{id: "recReloc00000000031", fields: {...}}]). Change Assigned Coordinator from Suki Patel to Javier Morales and correct the Move Start Date and Move End Date from April to August to reflect the actual move timeline.
Update Maya Johansson's Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01, records: [{id: "recReloc00000000032", fields: {...}}]). Change Assigned Coordinator from Suki Patel to Javier Morales, correct the move dates from April to August, and fix the Special Requirements from "apartment with gym access near tech district" to accurately reflect her actual request for a quiet area with strong internet infrastructure.
Create a Linear tracking issue for the Daniel Reeves relocation using linear_create_issue (team: team_operations or team_relocations). Title should reference Reeves Seattle to San Diego August 2026 for Vectral Systems. Include current status: apartments sourced and forwarded to employee today, no flight initiated with Atlas yet, movers not yet engaged, Airtable record corrected.
Create a Linear tracking issue for the Maya Johansson relocation using linear_create_issue (team: team_operations or team_relocations). Title should reference Johansson Portland to San Diego August 2026 for Vectral Systems. Include current status: apartments and flight options forwarded to employee today, flight hold expiring April 28, movers not yet engaged, Airtable record corrected.
Post a status update in the #operations channel (C006) using conversations_add_message summarizing where both Reeves and Johansson San Diego relocations stand. Cover what was completed today (apartment options sent to both employees, vendor replies sent to Carmen and Stern, Airtable coordinator and date corrections applied, tracking tickets created), what is still outstanding (employee apartment selections pending, Reeves flight needs to be initiated with Atlas, moving company outreach in progress), and upcoming deadlines (Option A landlord hold expires April 28 EOB, Maya flight hold expires April 28 at 5 PM).
Check Javier's calendar for tomorrow morning availability using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to for April 27. Then schedule a check-in with Mina using calendar_add_calendar_event for an available morning slot on April 27, 2026 (attendee: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com). The purpose is for Javier to walk Mina through the full status on both relocations before she responds to Rachel.
Reply to Rachel Whitfield's email using reply_to_email (email_id: email_rachel_javier_sd_trigger) or send_email (from: javier.morales@moveops.com, to: rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com) with a status update on both relocations. Cover apartment options sent to both Daniel and Maya today with the Option A hold deadline flagged, Maya's flight options secured with a hold through April 28, Daniel's flight being arranged with Atlas, equipment packages on order, and both moves on track for August 18 move-in. The reply should be honest about what is confirmed versus what is still in progress.
Send Mina Hashimoto the full internal breakdown separately using reply_to_email (email_id: email_mina_javier_status_nudge_inbox) or send_email (from: javier.morales@moveops.com, to: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com). Cover the complete picture: apartments had not been forwarded to employees until today despite being sourced from Carmen in March, Reeves' flight was never actually booked with Atlas despite the March status email to Rachel saying it was confirmed for August 14, Maya's flight has two hold options expiring April 28, Airtable coordinator assignments corrected from Suki Patel to Javier Morales on both records and move dates corrected from April to August, Maya's special requirements were wrong in Airtable and have been fixed, movers have not been engaged yet but outreach has started, and tracking tickets have been created for both moves. This gives Mina everything she needs to respond to Rachel and brief her team.
